altaria.notes: My ribbon master was an Altaria, but I actually came across this guy randomly while playing Scarlet.  I figured I'd leave this one in the dex so my ribbon master can have the honor of box 1 slot 1.  Alternatively, I could have used one of the many Swablu from the community day, but this was already evolved and in gen 9 and was easy to sort into place.

arceus.notes: The final proper rng with its own unique method.  I learned blink rng in gen 8 for this and it was a great moment to cap off the main shiny dex

arrokuda.notes: I wasted so much time on this.  I originally thought, since they were 2 star raids, I would have to hunt them elsewhere these since you can't access 2 start raids once you get access to water dens.  I thought I found one on land, but it was just bad data.  I eventually combed through Bulbapedia and found a raid den on land which spawns them in the dlc.  This pain made me build my own raid map tool, where I found out they do spawn in 3 star raids.  Oops - bad data made this more trouble than it should have been, but hopefully future generations will have my raid map tool to help

beldum.notes: This is an interesting line that took 3 different methods to complete.  I randomly found a Beldum in Go, and then there was an event for a shiny Metagross I dns tricked.  Once those were covered, I just left Metang in the air for a while.  Eventually, I caught it with a wild rng in Black.

celebi.notes: I actually had another one of these, but I traded it to complete the dex with shiny Eternatus.  This guy came after that.

ceruledge.notes: I think this was my luckiest sandwich hunt. 4 encounters!!!

chatot.notes: The workhorse of gen 4 and 5 rng for its ability to advance exactly 1 rng frame.  I actually used one in my casual playthrough of Black, but I didn't want to deprive that game of its Chatot so I just caught another one to fill the dex slot

cyclizar.notes: I found this while getting the 60 encounters for outbreak rates.  I hadn't even started hunting, and I'm glad it was fast since these guys are really hard to track down and catch since they move so quickly

delcatty.notes: So I used an rnged egg for my casual cute charm playthrough of Heartgold, but I didn't feel like transferring that one for the dex.  Instead, when a Skitty outbreak finally came, I caught 2 and evolved one for the dex, leaving the original in its home generation

deoxys.notes: This was like Mew, but I caught it in English Emerald.  I didn't even unlock the event - I just used ACE to warp to the island and then rnged it

diancie.notes: I dug up gen 6's event distribution software to get this since there isn't a dns trick method for old events on 3ds.  It was a fun experience

dratini.notes: This is actually Benga's Dratini gift.  I could have done the same for Gible in Black 2, but there was a community day so no need.

drifloon.notes: I got a few random shinies from PLA, but half of them were from this evolution line for some reason

dunsparce.notes: Spent way too long trying for the 1% spawn before realizing it's a common rock smash spawn

eelektross.notes: This guy came from a save file on a used game I bought.  Thank you IBRAHIM for your contribution to the shiny dex.

elekid.notes: I thought this was going to be an easy cute charm, but it doesn't appear in the wild in gen 4 unless you have FireRed for dual slot, which I don't.

eternatus.notes: Thank you to u/lexivine for trading this final shiny to me!

feraligatr.notes: Oho, so this one is actually a gen 4 egg rng unlike its coin case glitch fellows.  I was experimenting with it, and didn't know about the coin case glitch yet, so when I eventually did, it meant I had one fewer to do because of this experiment.

flabebe.notes: Man I thought these had both genders.  I messed up my gen 7 egg rng path because of these guys' fixed genders which made me turn to wild rng again

furfrou.notes: The difficulty in getting this in gen 7 wild rng is what originally made me give it up, but looking back, I should have kept with it instead of going to egg rng.  Once I figured it out, it wasn't so bad

genesect.notes: I bought Japanese Black 2 for this single mon since it was a Japan only event that gave these out as shinies

gengar.notes: I caught many Gengars totally unintentionally.  This one comes from a missed frame in a gen 7 rng I think from the abandoned supermarket which had Haunter's encounter slot instead of what I was going for, but I had already caught one in Platinum using dual slot but hadn't transferred it yet.  This one was closer to transfer, and so made it in the dex

illumise.notes: I have no idea where this came from, but I'm glad I had it because Volbeat was annoying to rng due to the weird gender thing specific to these 2 pokemon

jirachi.notes: This is the only pokemon created purely with ACE.  I could have rnged a Colosseum bonus disk but those are like 200 bucks and I didn't feel like it.  Instead, I did like 8 ACE codes to slowly build this pokemon into an identical copy from PID, IVs, valid OT TID and SIDs, the works.

kirlia.notes: This was a big accident.  I had another Kirlia, but I had evolved it to get a competitive Gardevoir for getting the master ball rank ribbon for my ribbon master.  This turned out to be a mistake since I had a Gardevoir already.  Oops.  Luckily, I still needed to rng a Gallade, so I farmed the den for another Kirlia while I was at it.

krokorok.notes: This was the first ever shiny I caught.  I was looking for a Trapinch in white 2 and found this beauty instead

lopunny.notes: This one predates my shiny dex.  I was going for getting max trainer stars in each generation and was looking for a Lopunny to mega for my Battle Maison team.  I didn't do rng then - I just dexnaved for good IVs and egg moves.  I ended up getting this shiny before I found one good enough

lunala.notes: This was a freebie from back in the day when you could sign up for this rewards program and get one of these shinies

malamar.notes: This is my casual playthrough Malamar from Ultra Sun.  After catching everything, I didn't need its hypnosis anymore and transferred it up

manaphy.notes: I could have played BDSP for this, but I had already performed a weird rng in gen 4 to make this shiny when the gifts were announced.  It's a weird one due to a bug that makes it shiny locked for the trainer that gets it, but not for anyone else, so you rng the egg for one game but accept the gift in another.  Then you trade and then hatch a shiny Manaphy

mew.notes: I bought a Japanese copy of Emerald just to make this one a bit more legit.  ACE to get the event item followed by an rng manip and shiny mew was mine!

mothim.notes: One of the few gender locked pokemon from gens 3 and 4 that I had to use egg rng for.  Metang at least was in the wild in gen 5, but this and Volbeat needed special care for getting the right rng frames in egg hatching

pelipper.notes: There was actually an event shiny Pelipper given out in gen 9, but I already had this guy when it happened

poipole.notes: This line and Type: Null were the scariest hunts.  They're NPC noisy, 1 frame windows in a 60 fps game you have to read clock hands and wait for to advance the rng.  The suffering in this hunt is what made me figure out gen 7 rng

probopass.notes: This guy was one of my casual team members in Platinum.  Unlike some others, it didn't have vital HMs and so I transferred it to rep its game here in the dex

raikou.notes: I remember getting these when they first came out.  Unfortunately, I lost that game, but thanks to the DNS trick, these come easy.  Good thing too, since roaming rng is annoying.

reuniclus.notes: This was one of the last shinies evolved, since I used this guy in a casual playthrough of Black 2.  I only played that game so I could have an unbroken Japanese transfer line up to bank from gen 3 which only became relevant at the very end when organizing all the pokemon in the dex

ribombee.notes: I was going to use one I rnged in gen 7, but this guy came in a Dynamax Adventure so I used it instead.  It's funny, the one I rnged in Ultra Sun wasn't intentional - I was going for Oricorio but forgot to adjust my settings.  Of course I ended up hitting the rng frame that time, so I named the Cutiefly that spawned Fate's Hand.  It had moved fate to make me select the wrong options and hit the right frame to make it appear and then made its replacement appear so it didn't have to leave its game

seedot.notes: My very first community day!  Didn't have any dreams of a shiny dex yet, but this was one of those things that got me hooked.

shedinja.notes: Yes, if you get a shiny Nincada to evolve, the Shedinja will be shiny too.

silvally.notes: Man this one was infuriating.  I hadn't gotten into just trusting the rhythm of the ticks and was trying to react.  On top of the NPC rng noise, this was my longest hunt which went over many days

staryu.notes: This is one of the lines I definitely thought had a gender before hunting via cute charm to no avail.  Eventually I did realize it and went back to the coin case glitch.

stunky.notes: Of all the gen 4 games I cute charmed, Diamond was not one of them.  Thus, I had to go all the way to gen 8 to get this line

tandemaus.notes: This was the first time I used the auto battle method of isolated encounter hunts because I can NOT tell this shiny.  Luckily, they came quickly.

thundurus.notes: Thank you Pokemon Go for your bug that let me catch these with high shiny rates without completing the quests.  It saved me quite a few rngs for these gen 5 legends.  Roaming rng is possible but annoying in gen 5, and gen 7 isn't great either, so thank goodness this guy came basically free

trevenant.notes: I wanted to try horde hunting to see if it was worth it and randomly got this guy in 3 Trevenant hordes.  It's a fine method, but I prefer rng's interactivity

tropius.notes: I could have put my cute charm playthough of Platinum Tropius here, but it had HMs and I didn't feel like deleting them, so I just caught another in the Great Marsh.

tyrogue.notes: Oh Tyrogue - one of the reasons I began tracking my catches at all.  I did 4 coin case glitches to get 4 of them for all the evos and then I realized after transferring that I already did them.  Oops!  Yeah, at first you just go for whatever shinies since the chances of forgetting and repeating are low, but it will happen.

vaporeon.notes: This guy is one of the only Pokemon Colosseum rngs I did and was the shiny Eevee starter that I took through that game for my casual playthrough.  I needed to do the rng so I knew my trainer id for making my ribbon master shiny, so I thought might as well get a shiny Eevee starter while I'm at it.

xurkitree.notes: This and Nihilego have really interest setups since they have npcs that wander the background so they may or may not be present.  This won't do for rng manipulation, so you have to do timed setups based on npc movement to guarantee 0 background npcs messing up the rng

zamazenta.notes: It was good luck these raids started in PoGo around when I was finishing the dex since I missed the event.  I did have to spend money on coins to do a bunch of remote raids on Poke Genie though

zeraora.notes: Thank you u/HieuReddit for trading this to me!

zubat.notes: Zubat's interesting - I actually caught a bunch of extras on accident.  I first rnged them in Emerald, but I also cute charmed them during the casual playthrough of each gen 4 game I did "just in case"

zweilous.notes: Man hunt Roaring Moon before hunting these guys since you're very likely to phase like I did on these Zweilous

zygarde.notes: Boy, am I glad I botted this - it's the hardest Dynamax Adventure and I got terrible luck
